title: NIS2 Art.23(3) — Cross-Border Coordination Task Tracking
industry: All sectors in NIS2 Annex I and II with multi-state operations
exclusions: Does not draft notification content, does not select the notification channel (encoded in the CSIRT directory lookup), does not handle EU-CyCLONe escalation under Art.16, and does not validate that the CSIRT directory is complete or current.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.34` row-per-task output, `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:saved_search` archive (7-year retention, RFC 3161 TSA-signed), `NIS2 Art.23 — Cross-border tasks` panel.

compliance.assurance_rationale: The UC tracks task-level evidence that the CSIRT liaison dispatched notifications to each affected member-state CSIRT after a cross-border assessment. Actual notification content, protocol selection, and follow-up are operational decisions outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves that for each cross-border significant incident, a notification task per affected member state was opened, assigned, and closed — with a traceable record of the notification channel used and the acknowledgement received.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.2.34` row-per-task output, `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:saved_search` archive. Dashboard panel `NIS2 Art.23 — Cross-border tasks`.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Fixture `sample-data/uc-22.2.34-fixture.json` injects a cross-border assessment artefact with `member_states_affected=["DE","FR","NL"]` at `_time=now()-50h`. ServiceNow has tasks for DE (state=3, ack received) and FR (state=2, open), but no task for NL. The search returns 2 rows: FR with `task_status=OPEN` `task_overdue=1`, NL with `task_status=NO_TASK` `task_overdue=1`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same fixture but ServiceNow has all three tasks in state=3 with `u_ack_received` populated. The search returns 0 rows.

dataSources: `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact stage=crossborder_assessment` for the assessment record with `member_states_affected`; `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item category=nis2_crossborder_notification` for notification tasks in ServiceNow (one task per member-state CSIRT); `index=phantom sourcetype=phantom:action_run action=send_csirt_notification` for SOAR-dispatched notifications. Lookup `nis2_csirt_directory.csv` mapping ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes to CSIRT name, notification channel, and expected acknowledgement SLA.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) ≥7.0; Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) ≥7.7; Splunk Add-on for Phantom (Splunkbase 3411) ≥3.0; Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621) ≥5.3.
spl:
index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact stage=crossborder_assessment earliest=-30d
| spath member_states_affected
| mvexpand member_states_affected
| rename member_states_affected as country_code
| lookup nis2_csirt_directory.csv country_code OUTPUT csirt_name notification_channel ack_sla_hours
| eval task_key = event_id . "_" . country_code
| join type=left task_key [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item category=nis2_crossborder_notification earliest=-30d
    | eval task_key = correlation_id . "_" . u_country_code
    | stats latest(state) as task_state, latest(sys_updated_on) as task_updated, latest(u_ack_received) as ack_received by task_key
  ]
| eval task_status = case(
    isnull(task_state), "NO_TASK",
    task_state IN ("1","2"), "OPEN",
    task_state = "3" AND isnull(ack_received), "CLOSED_NO_ACK",
    task_state = "3" AND isnotnull(ack_received), "COMPLETE",
    1==1, "UNKNOWN")
| eval hours_since_assessment = round((now() - _time) / 3600, 1)
| eval task_overdue = if(task_status IN ("NO_TASK","OPEN") AND hours_since_assessment > coalesce(ack_sla_hours, 48), 1, 0)
| where task_status != "COMPLETE"
| eval evidence_id = sha256(event_id . country_code . _time . "xb_task")
| table _time, event_id, country_code, csirt_name, notification_channel, task_status, hours_since_assessment, task_overdue, ack_received, evidence_id
| sort - task_overdue, - hours_since_assessment
description: For each cross-border significant incident with a completed Art.23(3) impact assessment, expands the `member_states_affected` list into per-country notification tasks, joins against ServiceNow ITSM to verify each task was opened, and flags tasks that are missing, open past SLA, or closed without acknowledgement from the target CSIRT.
value: Art.23(3) requires informing affected member-state CSIRTs — not just the operator's home CSIRT. Without per-country task tracking, the CSIRT liaison manually maintains a spreadsheet and inevitably misses one. This UC makes each country-task auditable and alerts when a notification channel has gone unacknowledged.
implementation: (1) Build and maintain `nis2_csirt_directory.csv` with one row per EU member state: country_code, csirt_name, notification_channel (MeliCERTes / email PGP / national portal), ack_sla_hours (default 48). (2) Configure ServiceNow request items with `category=nis2_crossborder_notification` and custom fields `u_country_code`, `u_ack_received`. (3) Schedule every 2 hours over 30 days. (4) Page CSIRT liaison on `task_overdue=1`; email digest on `NO_TASK`. (5) Throttle by `task_key` for 12 hours.
visualization: Geo-map: per-country task status (green=COMPLETE, amber=OPEN, red=NO_TASK/OVERDUE). Sortable table with deep links to ServiceNow. Single value: overdue tasks. Timeline: assessment-to-task latency.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Change where All_Changes.object_category="nis2_crossborder_notification" by All_Changes.object All_Changes.status All_Changes.user | rename All_Changes.* as * | eval task_key=object | lookup nis2_csirt_directory.csv country_code AS task_key OUTPUT csirt_name | where isnotnull(csirt_name)
dataModelAcceleration: Enable `Change` DMA for ServiceNow ITSM data; `Alerts` as in UC-22.2.1.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow on Splunkbase
Splunk Add-on for Phantom on Splunkbase

knownFalsePositives: 1) **Assessment lists a country the operator has since exited** — the entity scope shrank but the assessment pre-dates the scope change. Distinguish: lookup `nis2_entity_scope.csv` for the entity's current registered countries; the exited country will be absent. Suppress: filter `| lookup nis2_entity_scope.csv country_code OUTPUT in_scope | where in_scope="yes"`. 2) **CSIRT acknowledged verbally (phone call during a crisis) but the ServiceNow task wasn't updated.** Distinguish: task `state=Open` and `hours_since_assessment > ack_sla_hours` but IR shift notes mention verbal ack. Suppress: do NOT auto-suppress — page the CSIRT liaison to update `u_ack_received` in ServiceNow, because verbal-only acknowledgements are not auditable. 3) **Dual notification — both SOAR automation and manual email sent to the same CSIRT.** Distinguish: `index=phantom sourcetype=phantom:action_run action=send_csirt_notification country_code=<code>` shows a completed action AND a ServiceNow task exists. Suppress: the SPL already joins by `task_key` which deduplicates; if both SOAR and ITSM paths are active, treat ITSM as the source of truth. 4) **Non-EU member state listed in `member_states_affected`** — the assessment incorrectly included a non-EU country (e.g. UK, Switzerland). Distinguish: `nis2_csirt_directory.csv` does not have an entry for that `country_code`. Suppress: filter `| where isnotnull(csirt_name)`. 5) **ServiceNow batch import lag** — tasks created in ServiceNow but the modular input hasn't polled yet (default 5-min interval). Distinguish: `task_status=NO_TASK` AND `hours_since_assessment < 0.5`. Suppress: set a minimum age threshold of 30 min before surfacing NO_TASK findings.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, cross-border coordination task management)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, Assets & Identities for Member State enrichment)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Technology Add-on**: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928) must be installed on search heads and indexers. Verify with `| rest /services/apps/local | search label="Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow" | table label version`.
• **Indexes**: `audit_evidence`, `itsm` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `nis2_csirt_directory.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_csirt_directory.csv')`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.23(3)`. The control objective is: "Proves that for each cross-border significant incident, a notification task per affected member state was opened, assigned, and closed — with a traceable record of the notification channel used and the acknowledgement received." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is performed by the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928), landing events in `audit_evidence`, `itsm` under `evidence:nis2_artifact`, `snow:sc_req_item`. REST-polled ticket tables produce 500-5000 records per sync per table; at 300s interval expect ~3-8 GB/month for a mid-size service desk.

Configure the input through Settings > Data Inputs > ServiceNow Service Account. A reference `inputs.conf` stanza looks like:

```ini
[snow://{instance_slug}]
url = https://<instance>.service-now.com
table = {table_name}
interval = 300
index = itsm
sourcetype = {sourcetype}
```

The following fields are authoritative for this detection once the TA parses them: `number, state, priority, assignment_group, opened_at, closed_at, sys_updated_on, caller_id, category, subcategory`. Verify they are extracted at search time with `index=audit_evidence earliest=-15m | fieldsummary` before scheduling the detection.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Build and maintain `nis2_csirt_directory.csv` with one row per EU member state: country_code, csirt_name, notification_channel (MeliCERTes / email PGP / national portal), ack_sla_hours (default 48). The lookup table identified here is a long-lived compliance artifact; version it in source control and track every change with a pull-request review.

• **Hint 2:** Configure ServiceNow request items with `category=nis2_crossborder_notification` and custom fields `u_country_code`, `u_ack_received`. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 3:** Schedule every 2 hours over 30 days. Schedule cadence is a tradeoff between regulatory latency and search-head load; document the chosen cadence and the reasoning in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 4:** Page CSIRT liaison on `task_overdue=1`; email digest on `NO_TASK`. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

• **Hint 5:** Throttle by `task_key` for 12 hours. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Art.23(3) — Cross-Border Coordination Task Tracking," the aggregation produces the result shape required by the NIS2 control — each row is a summary record the regulation expects to be presentable at audit time.

This search correlates `index=audit_evidence` (sourcetype=`evidence:nis2_artifact`) on the outer side with `index=itsm` (sourcetype=`snow:sc_req_item`) on the inner side, joined on `task_key` across the last 30 days. `type=left` preserves all rows from the outer search; nulls in the inner side indicate missing data that may itself constitute a finding.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

**CIM / accelerated variant:** The detection also has an accelerated variant using `| tstats` against the CIM `Change` datamodel:

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Change where All_Changes.object_category="nis2_crossborder_notification" by All_Changes.object All_Changes.status All_Changes.user | rename All_Changes.* as * | eval task_key=object | lookup nis2_csirt_directory.csv country_code AS task_key OUTPUT csirt_name | where isnotnull(csirt_name)
```

When to prefer this variant: once the underlying datamodel is accelerated (verify in Settings > Data Models > `Change` > status=100%), this query runs 10-50x faster than the raw variant and can feasibly cover multi-month windows for audit reporting. When to avoid: if your field extractions are app-specific and the CIM tagging is incomplete, the `tstats` variant will silently under-count. Always compare raw vs accelerated counts on a known day before swapping the production schedule. Maintain both variants in the UC — the raw is the primary for accuracy, the accelerated is the performance optimisation for dashboarding.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.34: NIS2 Art.23(3) — Cross-Border Coordination Task Tracking]
cron_schedule = 0 3 1 * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.34,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `_time`, `event_id`, `country_code`, `csirt_name`, `notification_channel`, `task_status`, `hours_since_assessment`, `task_overdue`, `evidence_id`. Run:

```spl
index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table _time, event_id, country_code, csirt_name, notification_channel, task_status, hours_since_assessment, task_overdue
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(c) Lookup freshness:** The detection joins against `nis2_csirt_directory.csv`. Check its modification time and row count:

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_csirt_directory.csv
| eval _lookup_row=1
| stats count as rows
```

Compare the row count to the expected baseline; an unexpected drop is a sign the upstream export truncated.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.34: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Geo-Map: Per-Country Task Status (Green=Complete, Amber=Open, Red=No Task/Overdue). Sortable Table With Deep Links To Servicenow. Single Value: Overdue Tasks. Timeline: Assessment-To-Task Latency (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: When a cyber incident crosses national borders in Europe, we must tell the cybersecurity team in each affected country. This watch tracks a to-do list — one item per country — and makes sure every country's team gets notified and confirms receipt.
